item.title: Johan Gijsbert Verstolk van Soelen
item.description: Johan Gijsbert Verstolk van Soelen (16 March 1776 – 3 November 1845) was a Dutch politician. Between 1825 and 1841 he served as Minister of Foreign Affairs.

item.title: DFV-Supercup
item.description: The DFV-Supercup (also known as Pokal des Deutschen Sportechos)[1] was the super cup of East German football, played between the winners of the DDR-Oberliga and the FDGB-Pokal.

item.title: Ian Colletti
item.description: Ian Colletti (born March 3, 1997) is an American actor.[1] He is best known for his role as Eugene "Arseface" Root in the AMC television series Preacher.[2][3][4] He also played the teenage son of the title character on the American version of Rake. His younger sister is the actress Zoe Colletti.

item.title: Kazarki
item.description: Kazarki was a Khazar settlement. It was located west of the Volga River and north of Sarkel (probably in the present-day Penza Oblast), from roughly the 7th through the 10th centuries CE. The area around it was known in early Russian sources as Volost' Kazarskaya, or "Khazar region."[1]
